free_text: Gamma ray spectra from fast neutron interactions
6 MV Van de Graaf at Aldermaston.
gas targets contained in cylinders 2.5 cm long, at
one atmosphere pressure for tritium and 2 atmosphere
.Samples were cylindrical, 2 cm diameter and 2 cm
long, mounted on a light metal support.
Natural elements except for nitrogen in the form
of melamine (C3N6H6) and oxygen as H20.
Samples of water, Ba and W were canned in thin
brass, and Li-6 in aluminum (94.6% Li-6)
Pair spectrometer with three crystals, the
central one 2.5cm diam, 5.1 cm long with axis
towards the scattering sample though a 2.2cm diam
collimator. The other two crystals were 7.9cm diam
and 7.9 cm long with their common axis at 90 deg
sections for 4.43 MeV gamma production at 90 deg
sections renormalized in the light of more recent work.
corrected for line shape and detector efficiency,
from parameter that derived from elastic and
inelastic cross sections, density and atomic mass
statistical counting error is generally predominant
and accounts for practically all the error in
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Data were re-compiled from Tbl.3 in Subents 002-012.
MONIT-REF added. ERR-ANALYS update. DATA-ERR -> ERR-S.
Meaningless zeros were deleted from the data.
